title: The Book And The Ring
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Here were the end, had anything an end:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thus, lit and launched, up and up roared and soared
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A rocket, till the key oן¿½ the vault was reached,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And wide heaven held, a breathless minute-space,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In brilliant usurpature: thus caught spark,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Rushed to the height, and hung at full of fame
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Over menן¿½s upturned faces, ghastly thence,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Our glaring Guido: now decline must be.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In its explosion, you have seen his act,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ By my power may-be, judged it by your own,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or composite as good orbs prove, or crammed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With worse ingredients than the Wormwood Star.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The act, over and ended, falls and fades:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ What was once seen, grows what is now described,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then talked of, told about, a tinge the less
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In every fresh transmission; till it melts,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Trickles in silent orange or wan grey
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Across our memory, dies and leaves all dark,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And presently we find the stars again.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Follow the main streaks, meditate the mode
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of brightness, how it hastes to blend with black!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ After that February Twenty-two,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Since our salvation, Sixteen-Ninety-Eight,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of all reports that were, or may have been,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Concerning those the day killed or let live,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Four I count only. Take the first that comes.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A letter from a stranger, man of rank,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Venetian visitor at Rome, who knows,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On what pretence of busy idleness?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thus he begins on evening of that day.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Here are we at our end of Carnival;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Prodigious gaiety and monstrous mirth,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And constant shift of entertaining show:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½With influx, from each quarter of the globe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Of strangers nowise wishful to be last
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Iן¿½ the struggle for a good place presently
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½When that befalls, fate cannot long defer.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The old Pope totters on the verge oן¿½ the grave:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½You see, Malpichi understood far more
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Than Tozzi how to treat the ailments: age,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½No question, renders these inveterate.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Cardinal Spada, actual Minister,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Is possible Pope; I wager on his head,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Since those four entertainments of his niece
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Which set all Rome a-stare: Pope probably
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Though Colloredo has his backers too,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And San Cesario makes one doubt at times:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Altieri will be Chamberlain at most.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½A week ago the sun was warm like May,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And the old man took daily exercise
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Along the river-side; he loves to see
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½That Custom-house he built upon the bank,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½For, Naples-born, his tastes are maritime:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½But yesterday he had to keep in-doors
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Because of the outrageous rain that fell.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½On such days the good soul has fainting-fits,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Or lies in stupor, scarcely makes believe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Of minding business, fumbles at his beads.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½They say, the trust that keeps his heart alive
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Is that, by lasting till December next,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He may hold Jubilee a second time,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And, twice in one reign, ope the Holy Doors.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½By the way, somebody responsible
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Assures me that the King of France has writ
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Fresh orders: Fenelon will be condemned:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Cardinal makes a wry face enough,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Having a love for the delinquent: still,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Heן¿½s the ambassador, must press the point.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Have you a wager too dependent here?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now, from such matters to divert awhile,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Hear of to-dayן¿½s event which crowns the week,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Casts all the other wagers into shade.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tell Dandolo I owe him fifty drops
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Of heartן¿½s blood in the shape of gold zecchines!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Pope has done his worst: I have to pay
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½For the execution of the Count, by Jove!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Two days since, I reported him as safe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Re-echoing the conviction of all Rome:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Who could suspect the one deaf ear the Popeן¿½s?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½But prejudices grow insuperable,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And that old enmity to Austria, that
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Passion for France and Franceן¿½s pageant-king
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½(Of which, why pause to multiply the proofs
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now scandalously rife in Europeן¿½s mouth?)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½These fairly got the better in the man
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Of justice, prudence, and esprit de corps,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And he persisted in the butchery.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Also, ן¿½tis said that in his latest walk
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To that Dogana-by-the-Bank, he built,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The crowd, he suffers question, unrebuked,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Asked, ן¿½Whether murder was a privilege
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½ן¿½Only reserved for nobles like the Count?ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And he was ever mindful of the mob.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Martinez, the Cן¿½sarian Minister,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Who used his best endeavours to spare blood,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And strongly pleaded for the life ן¿½of one,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Urged he, ן¿½I may have dined at table with!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He will not soon forget the Popeן¿½s rebuff,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Feels the slight sensibly, I promise you!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And but for the dissuasion of two eyes
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½That make with him foul weather or fine day,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He had abstained, nor graced the spectacle:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½As it was, barely would he condescend
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Look forth from the palchetto where he sat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Under the Pincian: we shall hear of this!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The substituting, too, the Peopleן¿½s Square
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½For the out-oן¿½-the-way old quarter by the Bridge,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Was meant as a conciliatory sop
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To the mob; it gave one holiday the more.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½But the French Embassy might unfurl flag,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Still the good luck of France to fling a foe!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Cardinal Bouillon triumphs properly!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Palchetti were erected in the Place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And houses, at the edge of the Three Streets,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Let their front windows at six dollars each:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Anguisciola, that patron of the arts,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Hired one; our Envoy Contarini too.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now for the thing; no sooner the decree
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Gone forth, ן¿½tis four-and-twenty hours ago,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Than Acciaioli and Panciatichi,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Old friends, indeed compatriots of the man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Being pitched on as the couple properest
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To intimate the sentence yesternight,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Were closeted ere cock-crow with the Count.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½They both report their efforts to dispose
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The unhappy nobleman for ending well,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Despite the natural sense of injury,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Were crowned at last with a complete success:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And when the Company of Death arrived
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½At twenty-hours, the way they reckon here,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½We say, at sunset, after dinner-time,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Count was led down, hoisted up on car,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Last of the five, as heinousest, you know:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Yet they allowed one whole car to each man.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½His intrepidity, nay, nonchalance,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½As up he stood and down he sat himself,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Struck admiration into those who saw.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Then the procession started, took the way
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½From the New Prisons by the Pilgrimן¿½s Street,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The street of the Governo, Pasquinן¿½s Street,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½(Where was stuck up, ן¿½mid other epigrams,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½A quatrain . . . but of all that, presently!)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Place Navona, the Pantheonן¿½s Place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Place of the Column, last the Corsoן¿½s length,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And so debouched thence at Mannaiaן¿½s foot
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Iן¿½ the Place oן¿½ the People. As is evident,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½(Despite the malice, plainly meant, I fear,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½By this abrupt change of locality,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Squareן¿½s no such bad place to head and hang)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½We had the titillation as we sat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Assembled, (quality in conclave, ha?)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Of, minute after minute, some report
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½How the slow show was winding on its way.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now did a car run over, kill a man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Just opposite a pork-shop numbered Twelve:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And bitter were the outcries of the mob
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Against the Pope: for, but that he forbids
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Lottery, why, twelve were Tern Quatern!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now did a beggar by Saint Agnes, lame
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½From his youth up, recover use of leg,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Through prayer of Guido as he glanced that way:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½So that the crowd near crammed his hat with coin.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Thus was kept up excitement to the last,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Not an abrupt out-bolting, as of yore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½From Castle, over Bridge and on to block,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And so all ended ere you well could wink!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Guido was last to mount the scaffold-steps
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Here also, as atrociousest in crime.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½We hardly noticed how the peasants died,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½They dangled somehow soon to right and left,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And we remained all ears and eyes, could give
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Ourselves to Guido undividedly,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½As he harangued the multitude beneath.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He begged forgiveness on the part of God,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And fair construction of his act from men,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Whose suffrage he entreated for his soul,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Suggesting that we should forthwith repeat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½A Pater and an Ave, with the hymn
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Salve Regina Cן¿½li, for his sake.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Which said, he turned to the confessor, crossed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And reconciled himself, with decency,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Oft glancing at Saint Maryן¿½s opposite
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Where they possess, and showed in shrine to-day,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Blessed Umbilicus of our Lord,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½(A relic ן¿½tis believed no other church
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½In Rome can boast of) then rose up, as brisk
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Knelt down again, bent head, adapted neck,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And, with the name of Jesus on his lips,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Received the fatal blow.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The headsman showed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The head to the populace. Must I avouch
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½We strangers own to disappointment here?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Report pronounced him fully six feet high,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Youngish, considering his fifty years,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And, if not handsome, dignified at least.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Indeed, it was no face to please a wife!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½His friends say, this was caused by the costume:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He wore the dress he did the murder in,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½That is, a just-a-corps of russet serge,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Black camisole, coarse cloak of baracan
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½(So they style here the garb of goatן¿½s-hair cloth)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½White hat and cotton cap beneath, poor Count,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Preservative against the evening dews
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½During the journey from Arezzo. Well,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½So died the man, and so his end was peace;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Whence many a moral were to meditate.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Spada, you may bet Dandolo, is Pope!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now for the quatrain!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No, friend, this will do!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Youן¿½ve sputtered into sparks. What streak comes next?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A letter: Don Giacinto Arcangeli,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Doctor and Proctor, him I made you mark
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Buckle to business in his study late,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The virtuous sire, the valiant for the truth,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Acquaints his correspondent, Florentine,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ By name Cencini, advocate as well,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Socius and brother-in-the-devil to match,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A friend of Franceschini, anyhow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And knit up with the bowels of the case,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Acquaints him, (in this paper that I touch)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How their joint effort to obtain reprieve
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Guido had so nearly nicked the nine
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And ninety and one over, he would say,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At Tarocs, or succeeded, in our phrase.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To this Cenciniן¿½s care I owe the Book,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The yellow thing I take and toss once more
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How will it be, my four-yearsן¿½-intimate,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When thou and I part company anon?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Twas he, the ן¿½whole position of the case,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pleading and summary, were put before;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Discreetly in my Book he bound them all,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Adding some three epistles to the point.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Here is the first of these, part fresh as penned,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The sand, that dried the ink, not rubbed away,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Though penned the day whereof it tells the deed:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Part extant just as plainly, you know where,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Whence came the other stuff, went, you know how,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To make the ring thatן¿½s all but round and done.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Late they arrived, too late, egregious Sir,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Those same justificative points you urge
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Might benefit His Blessed Memory
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Count Guido Franceschini now with God:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Since the Court, to state things succinctly, styled
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Congregation of the Governor,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Having resolved on Tuesday last our cause
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Iן¿½ the guilty sense, with death for punishment,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Spite of all pleas by me deducible
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½In favour of said Blessed Memory,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I, with expenditure of pains enough,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Obtained a respite, leave to claim and prove
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Exemption from the lawן¿½s award, alleged
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The power and privilege oן¿½ the Clericate:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To which effect a courier was despatched.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½But ere an answer from Arezzo came,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Holiness of our Lord the Pope (prepare!)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Judging it inexpedient to postpone
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The execution of such sentence passed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Saw fit, by his particular chirograph,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To derogate, dispense with privilege,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And wink at any hurt accruing thence
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To Mother Church through damage of her son;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Also, to overpass and set aside
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½That other plea on score of tender age,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Put forth by me to do Pasquini good,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½One of the four in trouble with our friend.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½So that all five, to-day, have suffered death
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½With no distinction save in dying, he,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Decollated by way of privilege,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The rest hanged decently and in order. Thus
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Came the Count to his end of gallant man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Defunct in faith and exemplarity:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Nor shall the shield of his great House lose shine,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Nor its blue banner blush to red thereby.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½This, too, should yield sustainment to our hearts
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He had commiseration and respect
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½In his decease from universal Rome,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Quantum est hominum venustiorum,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The nice and cultivated everywhere:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Though, in respect of me his advocate,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Needs must I groan oן¿½er my debility,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Attribute the untoward event oן¿½ the strife
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To nothing but my own crass ignorance
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Which failed to set the valid reasons forth,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Find fit excuse: such is the fate of war!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½May God compensate us the direful blow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½By future blessings on his family
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Whereof I lowly beg the next commands;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Whereto, as humbly, I confirm myself . . . ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And so forth, follow name and place and date:
